title: Electronic device screen covers and electronic devices with bioactive materials
abstract: Certain embodiments described herein are directed to electronic device screen covers that include one more bioactive materials. In some embodiments, the electronic device screen covers can be used with capacitive touch screens on electronic devices such as cellular phones, tablets, laptops, kiosk displays or other electronic devices that include screens. Methods of using the screen covers and screens to reduce spread of infections are also described.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. An electronic device screen cover configured to couple to an electronic device screen, the electronic device screen cover comprising: a release liner; a first adhesive layer coupled to the release liner; a substrate coupled to the first adhesive layer; a second adhesive layer coupled to the substrate; a carrier support material coupled to the second adhesive layer; and a surface coating coupled to the carrier support material, the surface coating comprising a bioactive material to inactivate or kill bioorganisms that contact the surface coating. 2. The electronic device screen cover of claim 1, wherein the bioactive material comprises at least one of titanium, silver, copper and zinc. 3. The electronic device screen cover of claim 2, wherein the carrier support material comprises a polyurethane. 4. The electronic device screen cover of claim 1, wherein the bioactive material comprises a photocatalyst comprising titanium, and wherein the bioactive material comprises at least one additional transition metal. 5. The electronic device screen cover of claim 3, further comprising a second bioactive material embedded in the polyurethane of the carrier support material. 6. The electronic device screen cover of claim 5, wherein the bioactive material and the second bioactive material comprise different transition metals. 7. The electronic device screen cover of claim 3, wherein the first adhesive layer comprises a residue free adhesive. 8. The electronic device screen cover of claim 3, wherein the carrier support material comprises a substantially planar shape. 9. The electronic device screen cover of claim 8, wherein the electronic device screen cover is optically transparent. 10. The electronic device screen cover of claim 9, wherein the substrate comprises a polyolefin. 11. A method comprising placing one or more of the screen covers or screens described herein onto another article or device to facilitate transfer of infectious organisms, infectious virus, infectious viral agents or infectious viral particles from a human to the placed screen cover or screen, wherein the placed screen cover or screen comprises a bioactive material to kill or 

 inactivate the transferred infectious organisms, infectious virus, infectious viral agents or infectious viral particles. 58. The method of claim 57, wherein the virus that is transferred and inactivated is a coronavirus. 59. A method comprising reducing community spread of an infection by placing one or more of screen covers or screens described herein onto another article or device to facilitate transfer of infectious organisms, infectious virus, infectious viral agents or infectious viral particles from a user to the placed screen cover or screen, wherein the placed screen cover or screen comprises a bioactive material to kill or inactivate the transferred infectious organisms, infectious virus, infectious viral agents or infectious viral particles so successive humans touching the placed screen cover or screen do not become infected by the infectious organisms, infectious virus, infectious viral agents or infectious viral particles. 60. The method of claim 59, wherein the virus that is transferred and inactivated is a coronavirus. 61. A method of treating a human infected with an infectious organism, an infectious virus, an infectious viral agent or an infectious viral particle, the method comprising: administering to the infected human a therapeutic to treat the infection, and reducing spread of the infection from the infected human to third parties by placing one or more of the screen covers or screens described herein onto another article or device facilitate transfer of the infectious organisms, infectious virus, infectious viral agents or infectious viral particles from the infected human to the placed screen cover or screen, wherein the placed screen cover or screen comprises a bioactive material to kill or inactivate the transferred infectious organisms, infectious virus, infectious viral agents or infectious viral particles so successive humans touching the placed screen cover or screen do not become infected by the infectious organisms, infectious virus, infectious viral agents or infectious viral particles. 62. The method of claim 61, wherein the therapeutic is an antimicrobial agent. 63. The method of claim 61, wherein the therapeutic is an antiviral agent. 64. A kit comprising: a therapeutic to treat human infected with an infectious organism, an infectious virus, an infectious viral agent or an infectious viral particle; 

 a screen cover or screen comprising a bioactive material to kill or inactivate any infectious organisms, infectious virus, infectious viral agents or infectious viral particles transferred from the infected human so successive humans touching the placed screen cover or screen do not become infected by the infectious organisms, infectious virus, infectious viral agents or infectious viral particles. 65. The kit of claim 64, wherein the therapeutic is an antimicrobial agent. 66. The kit of claim 64, wherein the therapeutic is an antiviral agent. 67. The kit of claim 64, further comprising written or electronic instructions for using the therapeutic to treat the infection and using the screen cover or screen to prevent or reduce spread of the infection.
